title: Kubernetes Service Types
description:
## Choose the Right Service Type

You have a microservice running inside the cluster that needs to be reachable **from the internet** (external traffic). The cluster is hosted on a cloud provider (AWS/GCP/Azure).

Which **Service type** should you use?

options.text: ClusterIP — the default service type, only reachable within the cluster
NodePort — opens a port on every node, accessible from outside if the node IP is reachable
LoadBalancer — provisions a cloud load balancer and assigns an external IP automatically
ExternalName — maps the service to a DNS name, not suitable for external ingress

explanation: `LoadBalancer` is the right choice for production external traffic on cloud-hosted clusters. It provisions a cloud provider load balancer (like an AWS ALB or GCP L4 LB) and assigns an external IP. `NodePort` works in theory but requires knowing node IPs and is not production-grade. `ClusterIP` is internal-only. `ExternalName` is for mapping services to external DNS names.

hints.title: Recall Service types
hints.body: There are 4 service types: ClusterIP (internal), NodePort (node-level), LoadBalancer (cloud LB), ExternalName (DNS alias). Each builds on the previous in terms of exposure level.
